title: [ConstraintElim] Use ConstantRange information for ConstraintElimination
author: manasij7479
description:
Adds facts from calling computeConstantRange on the operands and operands of operands of comparisons.
Brings in range information explicitly specified in metadata or parameter attributes into the constraint system.

This enables ConstraintElimination to simplify more comparisons, helping SimplifyCFG and InstCombine.

https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/yBgMqx

original idea from @regehr

tests.test_body:
define i1 @arg_range_add_nuw(i32 range(i32 0, 10) %x, i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %w = add nuw i32 %x, %y
  %off = add nuw i32 %y, 9
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %z, %off
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %z, %w
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @neg_and_mask_tight(i32 noundef %x, i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %v = and i32 %x, 7
  %w = add nuw i32 %v, %y
  %off = add nuw i32 %y, 6
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %z, %off
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %z, %w
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @and_mask_add_nuw(i32 noundef %x, i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %v = and i32 %x, 7
  %w = add nuw i32 %v, %y
  %off = add nuw i32 %y, 7
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %z, %off
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %z, %w
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @or_add_nuw(i32 noundef %x, i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %v = or i32 %x, 1
  %w = add nuw i32 %v, %y
  %cmp = icmp uge i32 %z, %w
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %z, %y
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @or_lower_bound_offset(i32 noundef %x, i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %v = or i32 %x, 4
  %w = add nuw i32 %v, %y
  %off = add nuw i32 %y, 3
  %cmp = icmp ule i32 %z, %off
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %w, %z
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

declare i32 @get_percent()

define i1 @call_range_add_nuw(i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %v = call range(i32 0, 100) i32 @get_percent()
  %w = add nuw i32 %v, %y
  %off = add nuw i32 %y, 99
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %z, %off
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %z, %w
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @lshr_add_nuw(i32 noundef %x, i32 noundef %y, i32 noundef %z) {
entry:
  %v = lshr i32 %x, 2
  %w = add nuw i32 %v, %y
  %off = add nuw i32 %y, 1073741823
  %cmp = icmp ugt i32 %z, %off
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %r = icmp ugt i32 %z, %w
  ret i1 %r

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

declare void @use(i1)

define void @loop_iv_cond_variable_bound(i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %t.1 = icmp ule i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %t.1)
  %t.2 = icmp sge i32 %iv, 0
  call void @use(i1 %t.2)
  %t.3 = icmp sge i32 %iv, -1
  call void @use(i1 %t.3)
  %c.1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, %n
  call void @use(i1 %c.1)
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.2)
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv, %n
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 1
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

declare void @use(i1)

define void @loop_iv_cond_constant_bound() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %t.1 = icmp ule i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %t.1)
  %t.2 = icmp sge i32 %iv, 0
  call void @use(i1 %t.2)
  %t.3 = icmp sge i32 %iv, -1
  call void @use(i1 %t.3)
  %c.1 = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  call void @use(i1 %c.1)
  %c.2 = icmp ugt i32 %iv, 1
  call void @use(i1 %c.2)
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %iv, 2
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 1
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

declare void @use(i1)

define void @test_one_known_fact_false_branch(i8 %start, i8 %high) {
entry:
  %add.ptr.i = add nuw i8 %start, 3
  %c.1 = icmp uge i8 %add.ptr.i, %high
  br i1 %c.1, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:                                          ; preds = %entry
  ret void

if.end:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %t.0 = icmp ult i8 %start, %high
  call void @use(i1 %t.0)
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

declare void @use(i1)

define void @test_one_known_fact_true_branch(i8 %start, i8 %high) {
entry:
  %add.ptr.i = add nuw i8 %start, 3
  %c.1 = icmp ult i8 %add.ptr.i, %high
  br i1 %c.1, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:                                          ; preds = %entry
  %t.0 = icmp ult i8 %start, %high
  call void @use(i1 %t.0)
  ret void

if.end:                                           ; preds = %entry
  ret void
}

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Looks like the compile-time cost of doing this is very high: https://github.com/dtcxzyw/llvm-opt-benchmark/pull/3454#issuecomment-3881987708 (I expect this is due to the added constraints, not due to computeConstantRange.)

comments.author: manasij7479
artagnon
comments.body: The compile-time impact is due to computeKnownBits, and the additional compile-time from adding constraints is small: I already investigated this. If the compile-time impact is small enough, we can go forward with this, and later migrate it to use KnownBitsAnalysis, once that's ready.

comments.author: manasij7479
comments.body:
> This looks somewhat similar to @artagnon 's #176607. 
  
KnownBits is going to be slightly better for analyzing certain expressions, and ConstantRange another (overlapping) subset. Ideally we need both, but this has to be experimentally determined.

comments.author: manasij7479
comments.body:
> The compile-time impact is due to computeKnownBits, and the additional compile-time from adding constraints is small: I already investigated this. If the compile-time impact is small enough, we can go forward with this, and later migrate it to use KnownBitsAnalysis, once that's ready.

ConstantRange translates to precise linear constraints idiomatically, except for the disjunction implied by wrapped ranges.
KnownBits would not work as well for all ranges inferred from computeConstantRange, and vice versa. It would surely work for some of the tests I added though!
   
Maybe `ConstantRange::fromKnownBits(computeKnownBits(...))` could be a good idea? With your known bits caching approach, this could be reasonably fast.

comments.author: manasij7479
comments.body: Huh rebase did something weird. Fixed with a force push. Sorry about the noise!

comments.author: manasij7479
comments.body:
>  Would the current PR also allow remove `bool IsKnownNonNegative;`?

Doesn't seem so, at least not without extra plumbing. Maybe `IsKnownNonNegative` can be improved a tiny bit by also checking the range along with known bits, but it does not seem feasible to remove the constraints derived from `IsKnownNonNegative` in this pass.
